weather.summary: Saturday: 13°C, clear sky. Sunday: 13°C, few clouds

metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! I've found some amazing activities perfect for a 3-year-old, from exploring animals at city farms to crafting adventures at the Horniman Museum. With the lovely weather, it's a great time to enjoy outdoor activities!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: The Young V&A is a museum designed to inspire creativity in children, with interactive galleries and hands-on exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum has specific areas designed for toddlers and babies, including sensory experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Check out the Mini Museum, a sensory explosion for babies and toddlers.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small, beautifully designed playground featuring a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: The playground is especially designed for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford and walk through the Olympic Park.
transport.tip: Combine this with a visit to the Olympic Park's open spaces for a longer day out.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space and a story garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford. It's a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check the website for Baby Storytelling session times.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community city farm with a variety of animals including goats, chickens, and pigs.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers love seeing and interacting with animals, and the farm is easily accessible.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or cycle. It's centrally located in Hackney.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times to make the visit extra special.

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.description: One of the largest city farms in London, with a wide range of animals and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of open space for running around, plus many different animals to see.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Mudchute station.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy in the park.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: East London's most famous park. Has its own festival for little ones, with a full fortnight of free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Over Easter it practically has its own festival for little ones, with a full fortnight of free, daily, mostly no booking required activities that stretch from sports and fishing through to pond dipping.

transport.fromHackney: Several buses go directly to Victoria Park from Hackney.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for activity schedules.

activities.title: Leytonstone Library Kids Crafting
activities.description: Libraries provide great free activities for children. Leytonstone Library offers Kids Crafting
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers can engage in creative activities

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus to Leytonstone
transport.tip: Check local listings for specific schedule

activities.title: Urban Forest School
activities.description: An outdoor educational experience
activities.whyItWorks: Offers educational activities in nature setting

transport.fromHackney: Check website for locations in Hackney
transport.tip: book ahead

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: A museum with natural history displays, musical instruments, and gardens with animals.
activities.whyItWorks: There are farm animals, a music laboratory for children and beautiful gardens. Entry to the main museum and gardens is totally free.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill station.
transport.tip: Visit the Animal Walk to see alpacas, sheep, and goats.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur skeletons and interactive exhibits at one of London's most iconic museums.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum combines learning with fun, offering both educational and festive experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington).
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaur exhibits.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works through interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space where children can discover construction, water, light and sound through play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington).
transport.tip: Visit the Wonderlab for a hands-on experience (requires a ticket).

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world cultures and history with family activity trails and backpacks.
activities.whyItWorks: A dedicated family desk offers activity trails and museum backpacks to help explore the museum.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Tottenham Court Road or Holborn.
transport.tip: Pick up a family activity trail.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Offers a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo. Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a short bus ride to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Visit Battersea Children's Zoo

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: Offers a range of free family activities, including the Giant Chalkboard and WordPlay sessions for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Many free events are scheduled appropriate to age groups, making it easier to plan the day.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo station.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for free events.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Fun and educational free museum in London for kids
activities.whyItWorks: The All Hands Children's Gallery is specifically designed just for kids, so make sure to check it out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark station.
transport.tip: Check for “Character Encounters”, which will really bring your visit to life.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: London's highest public garden looks out over a 360° view of the city and it's a breath of fresh air that's absolutely free.
activities.whyItWorks: A visit to the Sky Garden is a great way to get a scenic view of London and the greenery and plants make it a sensory experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Monument Station
transport.tip: book in advance

activities.title: Little Angel Theatre
activities.description: Puppet shows adapted from Julia Donaldson's books, perfect for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Shows run about 45-50 minutes, making them ideal for toddlers' attention spans.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Islington.
transport.tip: The intimate space means your toddler won't feel lost in a sea of strangers.

activities.title: Polka Theatre
activities.description: Spring 2026 programme includes We're Going on a Bear Hunt, a musical stage version of Michael Rosen's beloved book.
activities.whyItWorks: Full of audience participation and live music, great for preschoolers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube and train to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Polka also has a café and buggy park, which sounds like a small detail until you've tried wrestling a pushchair into a Victorian theatre foyer.

activities.title: Regent's Park Open Air Theatre
activities.address: Inner Circle, Regent's Park, London NW1 4NU
activities.description: Sherlock Holmes (2 May to 6 June) gets things underway, with a thrilling new adventure directed by Sean Holmes
activities.whyItWorks: a stellar programme of shows including a mix of new productions and eagerly anticipated returns.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Baker Street
transport.tip: the theatre is just a 10-minute walk from the station.

activities.title: Family Sundays at the Royal Opera House
activities.description: Fun, relaxed and creative sessions perfect for families
activities.whyItWorks: relaxed and creative sessions perfect for families with children aged 3 to 10.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Covent Garden
transport.tip: Check website for schedule

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: Opened to glowing reviews and has quickly become a family favourite
activities.whyItWorks: A wholehearted and thoroughly charming homage to the iconic family films.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Covent Garden
transport.tip: Check website for schedule

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: A spectacular musical and plays, perfect for family entertainment.
activities.whyItWorks: Family-friendly shows like The Lion King offer immersive performances, colorful sets, and live music.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Covent Garden
transport.tip: Check website for schedule

activities.title: Matilda the Musical
activities.description: Family-friendly shows like Matilda offer immersive performances, colorful sets, and live music.
activities.whyItWorks: Family-friendly and enjoyable for the whole family.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Covent Garden
transport.tip: Check website for schedule

activities.title: Dino Disco for Kids
activities.description: Listen to family friendly tunes and dance among the dinos.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun and engaging way to experience the museum.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington
transport.tip: Check website for schedule
